Lineage Notes: Introduced in 2021.
Introduced in 2018. Secondary men's title on NXT.
One of WWE's oldest titles, introduced 1979. Various belt designs over the decades — all collapsed into this lineage.
Notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

Lineage Notes: Lineage traces back to WWWF Heavyweight Championship (1963). Unified WWE Championship and Universal Championship in 2023. Current design introduced 2023.
Raw-brand tag lineage. Traces back to the original WWF Tag Team titles. Includes Raw Tag Team Championship and World Tag Team Championship variants.
Notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

Lineage Notes: Introduced in 2023 as women's secondary title on NXT.
Originated in WCW (1975). Brought to WWE in 2001. Spinner variant (John Cena design) and '03-'20 design all part of this lineage.
SmackDown-brand tag lineage. Includes SmackDown Tag Team Championship and WWE Tag Team Championship variants ('02-'10, '10-'16).
NXT's top title. Includes designs from '12-'17, '17-'21, '22-'24, and current. All collapsed into one lineage.
New title introduced in 2024. No prior lineage.
SmackDown women's lineage. Includes SmackDown Women's Championship. Rebranded to Women's World Championship in 2023.
Notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

Lineage Notes: Exclusive to Saudi Arabia events. New in WWE 2K26.
Introduced in 2019 as the first women's tag title in WWE history. Cross-brand.
Notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

Lineage Notes: Reactivated in 2023 as separate title from WWE Championship. Lineage traces back to World Heavyweight Championship '02-'13 (originally WCW-derived).
Notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

Lineage Notes: NXT cruiserweight division title.
NXT's top women's title. Includes designs from '13-'17, '17-'21, '22-'24, and current.
New title introduced in 2024. No prior lineage.
Notes: Declared vacant at Hard Reset. Tournament to determine new champion in following weeks.

Lineage Notes: NXT tag title. Includes '13-'17 design and current design.
Raw women's lineage. Includes WWE Women's Championship '98-'10, Divas Championship, and Raw Women's Championship. Current design introduced 2023.
Active 1997-2001. Predecessor to the Cruiserweight division in WWE.
Active 2019-2023. Successor to the Hardcore Championship with 24/7 rules. Retired and replaced by Speed Championship.
Active 2001-2007 on SmackDown. Originated in WCW.
Custom design of the WWE Championship created for The Rock. Variant of the WWE Championship lineage.
Created by Ted DiBiase Sr. in 1989. Never an officially sanctioned WWE title — personal vanity belt.
Women's title of NXT UK brand (2018-2022). Retired when NXT UK folded.
Separate from NXT UK Championship. Introduced 2017, defended on NXT UK and UK tours.
Top title of NXT UK brand (2018-2022). Brand discontinued; title retired.
Custom design of the WWE Championship created for Stone Cold Steve Austin. Variant of the WWE Championship lineage.
Custom horror-themed design of the Universal Championship held by Bray Wyatt in 2019-2020.
Tag titles of NXT UK brand (2019-2022). Retired when NXT UK folded.
Active 1998-2002. Famous for 24/7 rule. Retired by Triple H.
Active 1997-2002. Unified with Intercontinental title by Triple H.
WCW's hardcore title, introduced late in the promotion's run.
Includes '91-'96 and main WCW World Tag Team Championship designs.
Secondary title in original ECW. Active through most of ECW's run in the 1990s.
Tag titles of original ECW promotion.
WCW's women's title. Largely inactive during most of WCW's run.
Includes WCW World Championship '91-'93, nWo World Championship, and nWo Legacy variants. Top title of WCW until 2001.
WCW's cruiserweight division title. Brought to WWE briefly after WCW merger.
Secondary WCW title, later absorbed into WWE's US title lineage after WCW merger.
Includes original ECW World Championship ('94-'01) and WWE's ECW revival versions ('06-'08, '08-'10). All collapsed into one lineage.
Top men's title in Lucha Libre AAA promotion. New addition in WWE 2K26 via Ringside Pass.
Top women's title in Lucha Libre AAA promotion. New addition in WWE 2K26 via Ringside Pass.
